content.text:
Here's what I found:

**Root cause:** Smoke tests `create-work-item-edge.test.ts` and `move-items-to-project.test.ts` pass `extractionModel: {} as any` to tool constructors. When `fireDescriptionUpdates` triggers `feature_created` and the target project already has 1+ description entries, `synthesizeDescription` calls `generateObject` with `{}` as the model — an empty object with no `specificationVersion`, `provider`, or `modelId`.

**Why it surfaces as this specific error:** The AI SDK's `resolveLanguageModel` checks `model.specificationVersion !== "v3" && !== "v2"`. Since `{}.specificationVersion` is `undefined`, it throws `UnsupportedModelVersionError` with all fields showing "undefined".

**Why it doesn't break tests:** All `fireDescriptionUpdates` calls are fire-and-forget (`void ... .catch(() => undefined)`), so the error is logged but swallowed.

**Fix applied:** Added a fail-fast guard in `synthesizeDescription` (`generate.ts:15-19`) that checks for a valid model before calling `generateObject`. This replaces the confusing AI SDK error with a clear message: `"Description synthesis requires a valid AI model but received object without specificationVersion"`.

The error is cosmetic in the current test setup since each test creates fresh projects (usually only 1 description entry, so synthesis isn't triggered). But when tests reuse projects or run in sequence where a project accumulates multiple triggers, this noisy error appears.
